---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Johan Zicola"
date: '2025-01-06'
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Objective

Di and Kathi proceeded to the RNA extraction and mRNA-seq library preparation of 8 different insect species. The libraries were sequenced in 100+100 PE mode by BGI.

The goal is to compare transcripts of different species and see if they are targeted by the designed dsRNAs used to kill the CSFB.

* Assemble the transcripts for each species
* Map the designed dsRNAs (50 or so designed) to these transcripts with allowed mismatches (do 0, 1, and 2)
* Get a list of candidate target transcripts

Check Cedden 2024 (https://resjournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/imb.12942) for the dsRNA design and off-target analysis. He developped an online tool to design dsRNAs (https://dsrip.uni-goettingen.de/efficiency).

## Data

Data are in `/usr/users/npghpc/data/NGS_data/BGI_data/F22FTSEUHT1495_LIBgsmfR_judi_8_insects`

Summary statistics for raw data:

| Sample | Length(bp) | TotalReads            |
|--------|------------|-----------------------|
| 01_Ad  | 100;100    |           64,769,548  |
| 02_Pm  | 100;100    |           56,305,457  |
| 03_Zt  | 100;100    |           50,054,875  |
| 04_Asp | 100;100    |           42,581,758  |
| 05_G   | 100;100    |           41,668,796  |
| 06_Ol  | 100;100    |           57,482,693  |
| 07_Pr  | 100;100    |           47,960,411  |
| 08_Ar  | 100;100    |           44,601,099  |

We have between 44 and 64 M paired-end reads.

fastqc

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
f *fq.gz > list_fastq.txt

while read i; do srun --partition medium fastqc $i -o fastqc_reports/ & done < list_fastq.txt
```


## Trimming

Remove 3' adapters from read 1 and read 2 and trim by quality, allowing no Ns.

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

#!/bin/bash
#
#SBATCH --job-name=trimming_cutadapt
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --ntasks=1 
#SBATCH --mem=16000
#SBATCH --output=slurm.%A.%a.out
#SBATCH --error=slurm.%A.%a.err
#SBATCH --partition=medium
#SBATCH --time=02:00:00
#SBATCH --constraint=scratch
#SBATCH --mail-type=END
#SBATCH --mail-user=johan.zicola@uni-goettingen.de
#SBATCH --array=1-8

module load cutadapt/4.4

file_name=$(find . -type f -name "*fq.gz" | \
cut -d_ -f1-4 | sort | uniq | sed -n ${SLURM_ARRAY_TASK_ID}p)

cutadapt -a AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCA \
    -A AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT \
    --quality-base 33 --max-n 0  -o ${file_name}_1.trimmed.fq \
    -p ${file_name}_2.trimmed.fq ${file_name}_1.fq.gz ${file_name}_2.fq.gz
    
```

The Ar sample shows a polyA adaptor contamination that is gradual from the beginning to the end of the read. It also contains a lower GC content than all other samples. 

Number of reads after trimming:

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
for i in *fq; do srun --partition medium fastqc $i -o fastqc_output/ & done

cut -f1,6 multiqc_general_stats.txt
```


| Sample      | TotalReads  |
|-------------|-------------|
| 01_Ad_L1_1  | 63,577,928  |
| 01_Ad_L1_2  | 63,577,928  |
| 02_Pm_L1_1  | 55,269,387  |
| 02_Pm_L1_2  | 55,269,387  |
| 03_Zt_L1_1  | 49,128,635  |
| 03_Zt_L1_2  | 49,128,635  |
| 04_Asp_L1_1 | 41,800,974  |
| 04_Asp_L1_2 | 41,800,974  |
| 05_G_L1_1   | 40,906,340  |
| 05_G_L1_2   | 40,906,340  |
| 06_Ol_L1_1  | 56,417,880  |
| 06_Ol_L1_2  | 56,417,880  |
| 07_Pr_L1_1  | 47,070,196  |
| 07_Pr_L1_2  | 47,070,196  |
| 08_Ar_L1_1  | 43,759,260  |
| 08_Ar_L1_2  | 43,759,260  |


# Trinity

See reference publication https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3875132/#S2

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

wget https://github.com/trinityrnaseq/trinityrnaseq/releases/download/Trinity-v2.15.2/trinityrnaseq-v2.15.2.FULL.tar.gz


tar -xzvf trinityrnaseq-v2.15.2.FULL.tar.gz

cd trinityrnaseq-v2.15.2

module load cmake

make

# Add to .bashrc
export PATH="${PATH}:/usr/users/zicola/bin/trinityrnaseq-v2.15.2"

```



Dependencies of Trinity were pre-installed on our HPC and loaded using [Module](https://modules.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html)

### Run


```{bash, eval=FALSE}

#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=test
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=00:00:05
#SBATCH --ntasks-per-node=1
#SBATCH --mem=10M
#SBATCH --partition=medium

echo $INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT
echo $INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT
echo $output_dir

```

```{bash, eval=FALSE}

sbatch  --export=INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_1.trimmed.fqX,INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_2.trimmed.fq,output_dir=trinity_output/Ad test.sh

```


```{bash, eval=FALSE}
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --job-name=Trinity
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --time=48:00:00
#SBATCH --ntasks-per-node=8
#SBATCH --mem=110gb
#SBATCH --partition=medium
#SBATCH --mail-type=END
#SBATCH --mail-user=johan.zicola@uni-goettingen.de
#SBATCH --output=Trinity.%J.out
#SBATCH --error=Trinity.%J.err

mem_bytes=$(</sys/fs/cgroup/memory/slurm/uid_${SLURM_JOB_UID}/job_${SLURM_JOB_ID}/memory.limit_in_bytes)
mem_gbytes=$(( $mem_bytes / 1024 **3 ))

echo "Starting at $(date)"
echo "Job submitted to the ${SLURM_JOB_PARTITION} partition, the default partition on ${SLURM_CLUSTER_NAME}"
echo "Job name: ${SLURM_JOB_NAME}, Job ID: ${SLURM_JOB_ID}"
echo "  I have ${SLURM_CPUS_ON_NODE} CPUs and ${mem_gbytes}GiB of RAM on compute node $(hostname)"

export PATH="$PATH:/usr/users/zicola/bin/trinityrnaseq-v2.15.2/"

module purge

module load samtools python/3.9.0 openjdk/17.0.0_35 salmon/0.10.2 bowtie/2.3.4.1 jellyfish/2.2.10 samtools/1.12

Trinity \
      --seqType fq \
      --max_memory 100G \
      --left $INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT \
      --right $INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT \
      --SS_lib_type RF \
      --output $OUTPUT_DIR \
      --CPU $SLURM_NTASKS_PER_NODE \

```

```{bash, eval=FALSE}
# generic command
sbatch  --export=INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT=X,INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT=Y,output_dir=trinity_output trinity_run.sh

# Ad
sbatch  --export=INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_1.trimmed.fq,INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_2.trimmed.fq,output_dir=trinity_output/Ad trinity_run.sh

# Pm
sbatch  --export=INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_1.trimmed.fq,INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_2.trimmed.fq,output_dir=trinity_output/Pm trinity_run.sh

# Zt
sbatch  --export=INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_1.trimmed.fq,INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_2.trimmed.fq,output_dir=trinity_output/Zt trinity_run.sh

# Asp
sbatch  --export=INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_1.trimmed.fq,INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_2.trimmed.fq,output_dir=trinity_output/Asp trinity_run.sh

# G
sbatch  --export=INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_1.trimmed.fq,INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_2.trimmed.fq,output_dir=trinity_output/G trinity_run.sh

# Ol
sbatch  --export=INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_1.trimmed.fq,INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_2.trimmed.fq,output_dir=trinity_output/Ol trinity_run.sh

# Pr
sbatch  --export=INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_1.trimmed.fq,INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_2.trimmed.fq,output_dir=trinity_output/Pr trinity_run.sh

# Ar
sbatch  --export=INPUT_FASTQ_LEFT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_1.trimmed.fq,INPUT_FASTQ_RIGHT=trimmed_reads/01_Ad_L1_2.trimmed.fq,output_dir=trinity_output/Ar trinity_run.sh

```



# Off-target analysis

Use Doga's script (https://figshare.com/articles/dataset/_i_in_silico_i_off-target_analysis_of_lethal_genes_in_cabbage_stem_flea_beetle/25283683?file=44671225)
